and practice daytrading and sizing with paper account
comment.raw_body:

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
note, that when you are using paper account, you literally do your best performance!
bcs there is no mental pressure.

practice trading in paper account until it becomes a routine: when to cut losses, when to go in a trade, how to size up, etc.
and when it is a routine: go out and start with low budget.
and go back to practice time to time...
even great traders do that sometimes...

it is like a training.. you don't always compete.. you train, practice.. and take rest periods as well

comment.body: to gauge potential move in last few minutes and also first minutes past 4:00.  Today was sell flow, but there was negative gamma 6225 and 6230. comment.body:
my problem with fibs (even tho i used them more than a decades ago, for one year i think, and it helped me in monhtly swings), that it is literally just guessing, with a coinflip.
ppl say that elliott wave is subjective, but it at least gives a system for the person to classify the market moves...
but fib retracements are literally jus coin flip guessing.
it only gives you a higher chance assumptions on a longer: monthly yearly timeframes, bcs of the logaritmic nature of growth, but nothing else
